per_turn_leaks.student_message: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, I get this really uncomfortable sensation after most bowel movements, like I need to go again even though I just did.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've had some achy pain in both knees and ankles that's been going on for a while now. It's not too bad, but it's definitely noticeable when I'm trying to move around or do things at work.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've had a low-grade fever a few times a week, nothing too high.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
My diet has been pretty irregular lately. With long work hours, I've been mostly relying on takeout and eating on the go. Exercise-wise, I don't really have much time for it with my job being so demanding. Usually, I try to squeeze in a short walk or some light stretching during breaks at work.

One thing that's changed recently is my drinking habits - I've noticed I'm having a few more beers than usual to help manage stress and unwind after a long day.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease about 10 years ago, and it's been a real challenge for her. My parents are both healthy.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister, she was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was around 30 years old. It's been tough on her, but she's managing it okay now.
